metadata.career_relevance: Feign Client is the standard for inter-service HTTP in Spring Cloud microservices. It's used at Flipkart, Swiggy, PhonePe, and every major Java microservices deployment. In interviews: 'How do you call another service?' в†’ Feign Client. It works alongside Eureka (automatic service discovery) and Resilience4j (circuit breakers). After this topic, inter-service calls are clean, type-safe, and testable.

hook.what_you_will_build: By the end of this topic, you'll declare Feign Clients as annotated interfaces, handle Feign errors with custom decoders, configure timeouts, and test Feign clients with WireMock.

concept_explanation.feign_setup:
**Setting up Spring Cloud OpenFeign:**

```xml
<!-- pom.xml вЂ” Spring Cloud dependency management: -->
<dependencyManagement>
    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-cloud-dependencies</artifactId>
            <version>2023.0.0</version>
            <type>pom</type>
            <scope>import</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>
</dependencyManagement>

<dependencies>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.springframework.cloud</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-cloud-starter-openfeign</artifactId>
    </dependency>
</dependencies>
```

```java
// Enable on the Application class:
@SpringBootApplication
@EnableFeignClients  // activates Feign client generation
public class OrderServiceApplication {
    public static void main(String[] args) { SpringApplication.run(OrderServiceApplication.class, args); }
}
```
concept_explanation.feign_configuration:
**Feign timeouts and logging:**

```properties
# application.properties вЂ” global Feign settings:
# Connect timeout (ms):
spring.cloud.openfeign.client.config.default.connect-timeout=5000
# Read timeout (ms):
spring.cloud.openfeign.client.config.default.read-timeout=5000

# Per-client timeout (overrides default):
spring.cloud.openfeign.client.config.market-service.connect-timeout=2000
spring.cloud.openfeign.client.config.market-service.read-timeout=3000

# Feign logging level (shows full request/response in DEBUG):
spring.cloud.openfeign.client.config.market-service.logger-level=FULL
logging.level.com.codequest.order.client.MarketServiceClient=DEBUG
```

**Feign logging levels:**
- `NONE`: no logging (default)
- `BASIC`: method, URL, status, time
- `HEADERS`: + request/response headers
- `FULL`: + request/response body (development only вЂ” verbose!)
concept_explanation.feign_vs_resttemplate:
**Feign vs RestTemplate вЂ” when to use which:**

```

в•‘                  в•‘ HTTP call)            в•‘ the contract)           в•‘

в•‘                  в•‘                       в•‘ @RequestParam вЂ” auto    в•‘
в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•¬в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•¬в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
в•‘ Error handling   в•‘ try-catch per method  в•‘ ErrorDecoder (once)     в•‘

в•‘ Eureka discovery в•‘ Manual URL            в•‘ name= в†’ automatic       в•‘

в• в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•¬в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•¬в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•Ј
в•‘ Best for         в•‘ Simple, few calls,    в•‘ Multiple endpoints,     в•‘
в•‘                  в•‘ non-standard HTTP     в•‘ standard REST           в•‘
в•љв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•©в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•©в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ќ

Recommendation: use Feign for most inter-service calls in microservices.
Keep RestTemplate for complex, non-standard HTTP interactions.
```

visual_aid.description: Two panels: (1) Feign Client generation at startup: Developer writes @FeignClient interface with @GetMapping methods. Feign reads annotations at runtime в†’ generates proxy class that makes real HTTP calls. Proxy implements the interface. OrderService injects the interface and calls methods as if local. Spring Cloud OpenFeign shown as the generator. (2) Feign request lifecycle: OrderService calls marketClient.getQuote('TCS','INR') в†’ Feign proxy intercepts в†’ builds URL (http://market-service/api/stocks/TCS/quote?currency=INR) в†’ sends HTTP GET в†’ receives JSON response в†’ Jackson deserializes to StockQuote в†’ returned to OrderService. Error path: 404 response в†’ ErrorDecoder в†’ SymbolNotFoundException thrown.
visual_aid.alt_text: Feign Client proxy generation and request lifecycle diagram

worked_example.scenario: Replace the RestTemplate calls in order-service with a Feign Client. The Feign interface declares the same operations with clean Spring MVC annotations. Add custom error handling.
worked_example.problem_statement:
Refactor order-service to use Feign:
1. Declare `MarketServiceClient` @FeignClient interface
2. Add @EnableFeignClients to Application
3. Write custom `MarketServiceErrorDecoder`
4. Update `TradeService` to use the Feign client (inject interface, not RestTemplate)
5. Test with WireMock
worked_example.expected_output:
# Startup:
INFO  - Generating Feign client for MarketServiceClient

# TradeService.executeTrade('TCS', 10):
# Feign proxy calls: GET http://localhost:8081/api/stocks/TCS/quote?currency=INR
# Receives: {"symbol":"TCS","price":3890.0,"tradeable":true}
# TradeOrder saved

# Error case (symbol not found):
# Feign receives 404 в†’ MarketFeignConfig.errorDecoder в†’ SymbolNotFoundException
things_to_notice: @FeignClient(name='market-service', url='${market.service.url}') вЂ” the name is used by Eureka in Topic 15.3. When url= is omitted and Eureka is on the classpath, Feign asks Eureka for the URL of 'market-service'. In this topic, url= overrides with a hardcoded URL.

The Feign interface uses the exact same Spring MVC annotations as @RestController вЂ” @GetMapping, @PathVariable, @RequestParam, @RequestBody. If you know how to write a controller, you know how to write a Feign client. The annotations have the same meaning but from the CALLER's perspective.

TradeService injects MarketServiceClient вЂ” an interface. Spring generates the implementation at startup (like how JpaRepository generates SQL from method names). The Feign proxy is a Spring bean, injected by type, tested with mocks.

The custom ErrorDecoder handles ALL errors for this client in ONE place. Compare to RestTemplate: try-catch per method, same exception handling repeated everywhere. ErrorDecoder: write once, applies to all methods of this @FeignClient.

WireMock (used in tests) is an HTTP mock server вЂ” it starts a real HTTP server on a test port and returns pre-configured responses. Unlike MockRestServiceServer (which intercepts RestTemplate), WireMock works at the HTTP level вЂ” compatible with Feign, RestTemplate, or any HTTP client.

industry_examples.use_case: Swiggy's order-service uses Feign clients to call restaurant-service, delivery-service, payment-service, and notification-service. The entire inter-service API for each downstream service is declared as a @FeignClient interface. When a new endpoint is added to restaurant-service, the order-service team adds one method to the RestaurantServiceClient interface. Clean, version-controlled contract in code.

PhonePe has separate services for fraud detection, bank routing, and reconciliation. Each is called via Feign. FraudServiceClient.checkTransaction(txnId) в†’ @GetMapping('/fraud/{id}') вЂ” looks like a local method. The ErrorDecoder maps FRAUD_DETECTED (409) to FraudException, DOWN (503) to FraudServiceUnavailableException. Circuit breaker (Resilience4j, Topic 15.4) handles the 503 case with a fallback.

When market-service moves from v1 to v2 API, the MarketServiceClient interface changes the URL: `@GetMapping('/api/v2/stocks/{symbol}')`. All callers update their Feign interface вЂ” the change is explicit, in one place, tracked in Git. With RestTemplate, the URL string might be scattered across multiple service methods вЂ” harder to find and update consistently.

applications.interview_relevance:
Feign Client interview questions:

- **'What is Feign Client and why use it?'** в†’ Declarative HTTP client. You declare a Java interface with Spring MVC annotations; Feign generates the HTTP implementation. Cleaner than RestTemplate for inter-service calls.
- **'How do you handle errors in Feign?'** в†’ Custom ErrorDecoder that maps HTTP status codes to exceptions. One class handles all errors for a client.
- **'What is @FeignClient(name=...) vs url=...?'** в†’ name is the service name registered in Eureka (used for discovery). url overrides Eureka with a specific URL (useful in dev or when not using discovery).
- **'How do you test a Feign client?'** в†’ WireMock: starts a real HTTP stub server. Point the @FeignClient url to localhost:WireMockPort.
applications.real_world_workflow:
Daily Feign Client workflow:

- New inter-service call: add a method to the existing @FeignClient interface
- New error scenario: add a case to the ErrorDecoder
- New service: create new @FeignClient interface
- Test: add WireMock stub for the new endpoint

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: Declaring @FeignClient interfaces without @EnableFeignClients вЂ” Spring doesn't generate the implementations, injection fails.
common_gaps.remediation: Add @EnableFeignClients to @SpringBootApplication (or a @Configuration class). Without it, Spring doesn't scan for @FeignClient interfaces and doesn't create the proxy beans. NoSuchBeanDefinitionException when trying to inject the interface.

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: Declaring the @FeignClient interface without public visibility вЂ” Feign can't generate a proxy for package-private interfaces.
common_gaps.remediation: Always declare @FeignClient interfaces as public. Package-private interfaces prevent Spring Cloud Feign from generating the proxy implementation.
common_gaps.detection_pattern: Application context fails to load; FeignException or BeanCreationException

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: Not handling Feign errors вЂ” a 404 from the downstream service causes FeignException (not a meaningful exception) that propagates as 500 to the caller.
common_gaps.remediation: Always add an ErrorDecoder for production @FeignClient interfaces. Map common status codes (404, 409, 503) to meaningful domain exceptions. The ErrorDecoder is registered in a @Configuration class and applied via the `configuration=` attribute on @FeignClient.
common_gaps.detection_pattern: FeignException.NotFound propagates to the HTTP response as 500

key_takeaways: @FeignClient(name='svc', url='${svc.url}'): declare the interface for a downstream service
@RequestHeader: add custom headers to Feign calls
Spring generates the proxy implementation at startup вЂ” inject interface by type
ErrorDecoder: maps HTTP status codes to domain exceptions вЂ” write once, covers all methods
WireMock: HTTP stub server for testing Feign (and any HTTP client)
Feign > RestTemplate for: standard REST, many endpoints, Eureka integration
RestTemplate > Feign for: complex non-standard HTTP, reactive code

self_check_questions.question: You call userClient.getUser('U999') via Feign. user-service returns 404. Your API returns 500. How do you fix it?
self_check_questions.answer: Add an ErrorDecoder to the Feign configuration: `if (status == 404) return new UserNotFoundException('User not found: U999')`. Then add @ExceptionHandler(UserNotFoundException.class) in @RestControllerAdvice to return 404 with a structured error body. Without ErrorDecoder, Feign wraps the 404 in a FeignException which propagates as 500. The ErrorDecoder translates HTTP status codes into meaningful domain exceptions вЂ” then your GlobalExceptionHandler handles them.

self_check_questions.question: MarketServiceClient is a @FeignClient interface. Can you inject it directly into a test as @Autowired? What do you need in the test configuration?
self_check_questions.answer: Yes вЂ” Feign generates a concrete implementation at startup, which is a regular Spring bean. In @SpringBootTest, @Autowired MarketServiceClient works. But the Feign client will try to call the real URL (localhost:8081). For tests, either: (1) use WireMock to start a stub HTTP server and point @FeignClient url= to the WireMock port, (2) use @MockBean MarketServiceClient in @WebMvcTest (replaces Feign with a Mockito mock), or (3) override the url in test application.properties. @SpringBootTest(NONE) with WireMock is the most realistic.

self_check_questions.question: What is the difference between @FeignClient(name='market-service') and @FeignClient(name='market-service', url='http://localhost:8081')?
self_check_questions.answer: Without url=: Feign uses Eureka (service discovery) to find the URL for 'market-service'. It queries Eureka registry for instances of the service named 'market-service' and load-balances across them. With url=: Feign uses the hardcoded URL, bypassing service discovery entirely. url= is used in development (no Eureka set up yet) or for services external to the cluster. In Topic 15.3 (Eureka), you'll remove url= and let Feign find services by name automatically.

reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: **Eureka Service Discovery** вЂ” right now, Feign client URLs are hardcoded (url='http://localhost:8081'). In production, services run on dynamic IPs in Kubernetes вЂ” you can't hardcode addresses. Eureka is a service registry: each microservice registers itself on startup ('I'm market-service at 10.0.1.45:8081'). Feign clients query Eureka: 'Where is market-service?' в†’ gets the IP. When services scale to multiple instances, Feign load-balances across them automatically. After Topic 15.3, services find each other by name вЂ” no hardcoded URLs anywhere.
